<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The error says line 103, column 48. Line 103 is:
```
(groupName: string): string | undefined => selectedByGroup.get(groupName),
```

Column 48 would be around `selectedByGroup.get(groupName)`. The issue is that `Map.get()` returns `V | undefined` where V is the value type of the Map. The Map is `Map<string, string>`, so `get` should return `string | undefined`. 

But wait - maybe the issue is that `selectedByGroup` is inferred in a way that makes `.get()` return `unknown`? I will check how `selectedByGroup` is created. It comes from the useMemo above - it returns `new Map<string, string>()` or `new Map(...)`. So the Map should be `Map<string, string>`. 

Oh, I see - when you do `new Map(...)` with `.map((x: { name: string; now: string }) => [x.name, x.now])`, TypeScript might infer the Map as `Map<string, string>` but in strict mode, the array `[x.name, x.now]` could be inferred as a tuple and the Map could end up with a different type. I will look at the full useMemo again.

Actually re-reading the error: "Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'string | undefined'". So something is `unknown` and we're trying to assign it to `string | undefined`. The callback returns `selectedByGroup.get(groupName)`. So either:
1. `selectedByGroup.get(groupName)` is returning `unknown` - which could happen if `selectedByGroup` is typed as `Map<string, unknown>` or similar
2. Or the whole callback's return type is being inferred as unknown

I will look at the beginning of the file to see how selectedByGroup and the types are set up.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The `selectedByGroup` useMemo returns either `new Map<string, string>()` or `new Map(array)`. When you pass an array of `[string, string][]` to `new Map()`, TypeScript might infer it as `Map<string, string>`. But the issue could be that the `.map()` returns `[string, string][]` and when you pass that to `new Map()`, in some TypeScript versions or with certain configs, the Map might be inferred as `Map<string, unknown>` or the `.get()` might return `unknown`.

Actually re-reading the error again: "error TS2322: Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'string | undefined'."

So the return type of the function is declared as `string | undefined`, and the expression `selectedByGroup.get(groupName)` is being inferred as `unknown`. So `Map.get()` is returning `unknown`. This happens when the Map is typed as `Map<string, unknown>` or when the Map's value type is unknown.

The fix would be to ensure the callback returns `string | undefined` by either:
1. Adding a type assertion: `selectedByGroup.get(groupName) as string | undefined`
2. Or ensuring selectedByGroup is properly typed as Map<string, string>

Looking at the useMemo again - when we do `new Map(g.filter(...).map(...))`, the map callback returns `[x.name, x.now]` which TypeScript might infer as `(string | undefined)[]` or similar if it's not sure about the types. So the Map could end up being `Map<string, string>` or in strict mode maybe the inference goes wrong.

The safest fix is to explicitly type the return value of getSelectedForGroup or add a type assertion. I will add an explicit type assertion to make the build pass: `return selectedByGroup.get(groupName) ?? undefined` or we could do `return selectedByGroup.get(groupName) as string | undefined`.

Actually, the cleanest fix is to ensure the Map is correctly typed. We can add an explicit type to the useMemo:
`return new Map<string, string>(...)`

That way selectedByGroup will be Map<string, string> and .get() will return string | undefined. I will try that first. If the issue is that the array passed to Map is inferred as [string, unknown][] or something, we need to fix the map callback.

Looking at the code again:
```javascript
return new Map(
  g
    .filter(
      (x: { name?: string; now?: string }) =>
        x.name != null && x.now != null,
    )
    .map((x: { name: string; now: string }) => [x.name, x.now]),
);
```

The .map returns [x.name, x.now] which is [string, string]. So we get [string, string][]. When you do new Map([string, string][]), TypeScript should infer Map<string, string>. Unless... in strict mode or with noImplicitAny, maybe the issue is different.

I will try adding explicit type parameter to Map: `new Map<string, string>(...)` - that should fix it.

Or the issue might be that `groups` has type that makes `g` have elements of a type where `name` and `now` are unknown. So when we do x.name and x.now, they could be unknown. So the Map ends up as Map<unknown, unknown> or Map<string, unknown>. So selectedByGroup.get(groupName) returns unknown.

So the fix is to explicitly type the Map as Map<string, string>:
```javascript
return new Map<string, string>(
  g
    .filter(...)
    .map((x: { name: string; now: string }) => [x.name, x.now] as [string, string]),
);
```

Or we could add `as [string, string]` to the tuple to help inference. Or simply type the useMemo return: `useMemo((): Map<string, string> => {...}, [groups])`.

I will do the useMemo return type approach - that's the cleanest.
</details></think>
